examples: No, if you haven't lived through 1998, you don't know what sanctimony is.
Handguns should be licensed, but that hardly excuses the convenient sanctimony of blaming the crime on the weapon, rather than on what resides in the human mind.
He used his public comments to raise anxieties and pour on the sanctimony.
That incident subjected the United States to mindless sanctimony and vitriol from Europe.
It is no surprise, perhaps, that the News of the World has cloaked its exposure of Mr Dallaglio in sanctimony.
